signature.trigger: Multiple rectangular bordered regions containing dark-grey interior with colored marker pixels (off-black/white dots) visible in a grid layout
signature.evidence: ls20 level 1: Two bordered panels in dark-grey area contain off-black/white pixel patterns. A yellow horizontal bar runs across the bottom. Blue/orange/red small squares appear near edges.

abstraction.summary: Bordered panels (dark border, grey interior) display patterns made of colored markers. A separate bottom region shows a horizontal bar (score/status). Small colored squares near border edges may be controls or indicators.

evidence_history.evidence: ls20 level 1: Two bordered panels in dark-grey area contain off-black/white pixel patterns. A yellow horizontal bar runs across the bottom. Blue/orange/red small squares appear near edges.

signature.trigger: Blue and orange objects move vertically in response to up/down actions; a yellow horizontal bar shrinks with each action used.
signature.evidence: ACTION1: blue(centroid 48->43) and orange(46->40) both moved up 5 rows; yellow bar size 84->82. ACTION2: blue(43->48) and orange(40->46) moved down 5 rows; yellow bar 82->80. Two objects move together as a unit.

abstraction.summary: ACTION1 moves blue+orange objects up by 5 rows; ACTION2 moves them down by 5 rows. Each action costs 1 step (yellow bar shrinks by 1 unit). Goal likely involves aligning the sliding objects with a target position within the step budget.
abstraction.hint: Track the target position (likely a gap or marker in the dark-grey area) and use the minimum number of up/down actions to align the blue/orange slider with it.

evidence_history.evidence: ACTION1: blue(centroid 48->43) and orange(46->40) both moved up 5 rows; yellow bar size 84->82. ACTION2: blue(43->48) and orange(40->46) moved down 5 rows; yellow bar 82->80. Two objects move together as a unit.

signature.trigger: A movable object (blue+orange) can slide in all 4 directions by 5 cells per action; a target pattern exists in a bordered box; a step counter (yellow bar) shrinks each action.
signature.evidence: diff_last_frames confirmed: ACTION1 moves color9(blue) and color12(orange) up by 5r; ACTION2 down 5r; ACTION3 left 5c; ACTION4 right 5c. Yellow bar shrinks by 2px width per action. A bordered box in upper right (rows 8-16, cols 32-40) shows a target pattern with blue(9) pixels.

abstraction.summary: ACTION1=up, ACTION2=down, ACTION3=left, ACTION4=right — each moves the blue/orange slider 5 cells in that direction. The goal is to align the slider with the target position shown in a bordered box (likely in the upper area). Yellow bar tracks remaining budget.
abstraction.hint: Calculate the offset between current slider position and target, divide by 5 to find number of actions needed in each axis. Use minimum actions to conserve step budget.

abstraction.summary: A yellow bar acts as a decreasing step counter and a dark-grey bar acts as an increasing progress indicator. Each action consumes 2 units from yellow and adds 2 to dark-grey. When yellow is exhausted the level ends.
abstraction.hint: The yellow bar size divided by 2 gives remaining steps. Prioritize goal actions before yellow is depleted.

evidence_history.evidence: Over 42+ actions, yellow bar shrank from size 84 to 48 (each action -2px width), dark-grey grew from size 2 to 36 correspondingly. Both run along row 62.
